title: Dungeons of Draggmar - Amazing Art, and Initial Frustrations
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3gPoLb5rT70

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes a preliminary look at Dungeons of Draggmar from The Mongrel — and it's a tale of two halves. The art is what drew him to back it, and it's spectacular: a small card-based crawl in the vein of Darkest Dungeon where you play a Guild Master hiring and equipping heroes, then sending them to probable death across three dungeon levels for fame. He gushes over the heroes, items and dungeon-room cards and wishes the art were in a better game.

Because — and he stresses this isn't a review, just first frustrations — learning it has been miserable. The included rulebook is microscopic (he printed a larger one), the rules are unclear with no examples, and both his learning games left him with no meaningful choices: drawing no weapons, he could only arrange cards to block damage before dying, with zero agency. He suspects he may be playing wrong (a new rulebook was promised on BGG) but the experience soured him. He also rails against the wrinkly neoprene mats that won't lie flat even after ironing. Gorgeous art, frustrating game — so far.

----

Daniel says Draggmar's art deserves a better game, and a bad first session made him want to shelve it. How much does a rough learning experience colour your verdict — can great art buy a game a second chance?
